newsBriefing: It was a landmark day across the sports world, headlined by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander eclipsing Wilt Chamberlain’s 63-year-old NBA record with his 127th consecutive 20-point game and Luka Doncic erupting for 51 points in LeBron James’ return to the Lakers' lineup. The NFL landscape shifted significantly as the Vikings secured Kyler Murray on a veteran minimum deal following his release from the Cardinals, while the Cowboys integrated newly acquired pass rusher Rashan Gary amidst a "crazy" week of trades and social media hacks. However, the day was also clouded by serious developments, including Atlanta Falcons edge rusher James Pearce Jr. facing multiple felony charges and Toronto’s Auston Matthews exiting a game with a knee injury after a controversial hit. In other arenas, Dwight Howard officially retired from all basketball following his Hall of Fame induction, Rory McIlroy struggled early at the Players Championship while a pack of five leaders set the pace at 5-under, and Kansas State neared a deal to name Belmont's Casey Alexander as their next head coach. Moving into postseason play, Team USA revamped its pitching roster for the World Baseball Classic quarterfinals, and the Big 12 made the unprecedented mid-tournament decision to scrap its new LED glass court for traditional hardwood after widespread player complaints about dangerous slippage.
